entries.title: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
entries.snippet: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

entries.title: No 10 says Falklands sovereignty rests with UK after report of US 'review'
entries.snippet: An internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of a change in position in retaliation for the UK not joining the Iran war.

entries.title: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
entries.snippet: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

entries.title: South Africa's top envoy to Ghana summoned over attacks on foreigners
entries.snippet: A Ghanaian national was confronted by South Africans in a viral clip and questioned about his immigration status.

entries.title: Poisoning suspected in deaths of 18 wolves in Italian national park
entries.snippet: After the carcasses of 10 wolves were found last week, another eight have been found in recent days.

entries.title: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
entries.snippet: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

entries.title: Epstein housed abuse victims in London flats, BBC reveals
entries.snippet: The revelations intensify concerns about repeated police decisions not to investigate 2015 trafficking claims.

entries.title: Kenyan leader sparks uproar after mocking Nigerians' spoken English
entries.snippet: William Ruto says Kenyans speak "some of the best English in the world" while it is hard to understand Nigerians.

entries.title: South Korea police arrest man for posting AI photo of runaway wolf
entries.snippet: The widely circulated image had prompted authorities to move their search operation.

entries.title: US soldier charged after winning $400,000 betting on removal of Maduro
entries.snippet: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly made trades on Polymarket on the basis of classified information, the justice department says.

entries.title: Woman trapped in poo for three hours after outback toilet collapses
entries.snippet: The woman got stuck waist-deep in a pit latrine in the Australian outback after it gave way.

entries.title: UK biobank data incident caused by 'a few bad apples', boss says
entries.snippet: Professor Sir Rory Collins said he was "angry" and "upset" about the incident, as both the bank's boss and a participant.

entries.title: Judge dismisses Smokey Robinson's defamation claim against rape accusers
entries.snippet: The veteran singer had made a counterclaim after four housekeepers sued him for alleged sexual assault.

entries.title: British conwoman who claimed to be royalty hit with new charges in Singapore
entries.snippet: Dionne Marie Hanna, 85, is accused of duping more than two dozen people - including her own son.

entries.title: Aboriginal children's book pulled over illustrator's Bondi attack comments
entries.snippet: Several writers have cut ties with the University of Queensland Press for cancelling the book.

entries.title: Huge chunk of glacier blocks Everest route in peak climbing season
entries.snippet: Sherpas cannot prepare the route to the world's highest peak because a huge block of ice is in the way.

entries.title: Ringo Starr: 'I made all my mistakes on stage'
entries.snippet: Beatles star reveals all about his new country album, being a Sir and coming up with the phrase A Hard Day's Night.

entries.title: Singer D4vd had 'significant amount' of child sex abuse images when arrested, prosecutors say
entries.snippet: The Tiktok star's attorneys have maintained his innocence and vowed to "vigorously defend" him.

entries.title: Meta to cut one in 10 jobs after spending billions on AI
entries.snippet: The cuts, which employees had been expecting for weeks, will be Meta's largest layoff since 2023.

entries.title: White House memo claims mass AI theft by Chinese firms
entries.snippet: A memo from Michael Kratsios says firms, mainly in China, are wrongfully distilling US AI models.

entries.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire is extended by 3 weeks as tensions rise in Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: Hezbollah and Israel traded fire just hours after the ceasefire extension was announced, underscoring its fragility.

entries.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire. And, Trump eases medical marijuana rules
entries.snippet: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks, President Trump says. And, the Trump administration is easing rules on medical marijuana.

entries.title: Thousands of seafarers stranded by ongoing U.S. blockade on Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports drags on, thousands of seafarers are stranded on ships, and economic shockwaves ripple around the world.

entries.title: 'Self-aware' robots can learn complex tasks by watching humans. Is that a good thing?
entries.snippet: Scientists say they've made a key breakthrough that would allow robots to figure out complex tasks on their own, but experts say it raises questions about how much risk comes with letting robots be in charge of their own learning.

entries.title: After 2 failed votes, Mike Johnson unveils new plan to extend key U.S. spy powers
entries.snippet: With an April 30 deadline fast approaching, Johnson unveiled his latest proposal to extend the controversial surveillance program known as FISA 702.

entries.title: Why Trump wants to spend $1 billion on Great Salt Lake
entries.snippet: Utah's Great Salt Lake has been labeled an "environmental nuclear bomb" and it has the attention of the president of the United States.

entries.title: Morning news brief
entries.snippet: Ongoing U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz strands thousands of seafarers, Trump administration eases rules on medical marijuana, Wildfires fueled by drought continue to spread in parts of Georgia.

entries.title: DHS starts deportation of woman whose loved one died on Baltimore's Key Bridge
entries.snippet: The mother's story is a stark example of how immigration policy can change drastically with each administration — and transform the lives of immigrants.

entries.title: Cuba's ambassador to Mexico says U.S. is to blame for island's crisis
entries.snippet: Even as the U.S. threatens military action, Cuba's ambassador to Mexico blames Washington for the country's crisis and says Cuba is prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.

entries.title: Two widows share how a park bench brought them together
entries.snippet: Two widows discuss their friendship which started on a park bench

entries.title: Judge sets date for evidence against singer D4vd in killing of 14-year-old to be presented
entries.snippet: Lawyers for singer D4vd insisted Thursday on forcing prosecutors to show what evidence they have, and a judge set a hearing for next week for them to lay it out publicly in the killing and dismemberment of Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

entries.title: U.S. soldier charged with using classified intel to win more than $400K in bet on Maduro raid
entries.snippet: Gannon Ken Van Dyke was part of the operation to capture Maduro in January and used his access to classified information to make money on the prediction market site Polymarket, the federal prosecutor's office in New York said.

entries.title: WATCH: Trump announces Israel and Lebanon have agreed to 3-week ceasefire extension
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah by three weeks after talks at the White House on Thursday.

entries.title: Kalshi fines and suspends 3 congressional candidates for betting on their own elections
entries.snippet: Three congressional candidates wagered on the outcome of their own elections on Kalshi, according to the prediction market, which said Wednesday that it fined and suspended the men from their platform for five years.

entries.title: Trump in no rush to set deadline on Iran ceasefire extension
entries.snippet: Two days after President Trump extended his two-week ceasefire with Iran, the prospect of talks is giving way to more tensions and brinksmanship from both sides. Iran released video of forces taking control of two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, and President Trump wrote that he

entries.title: WATCH: Trump unveils deal with Regeneron to lower its drug prices for Medicaid
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump on Thursday announced a deal with drugmaker Regeneron to lower the cost of its pharmaceutical products as part of the White House's signature drug pricing initiative.

entries.title: Israel's UN ambassador says IDF is 'most moral military in the world'
entries.snippet: Officials from Israel and Lebanon met in Washington to build on last week's agreement for a 10-day ceasefire. Both sides pledged to halt offensive operations, while Lebanon committed to taking meaningful steps to prevent Hezbollah from launching attacks on Israel. Geoff Bennett s

entries.title: News Wrap: Wildfires threaten homes in southern Georgia
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Thursday, hundreds in Georgia have fled their homes as wildfires threaten areas in the southern part of the state, Warner Bros. shareholders voted to approve the company's $81 billion sale to Paramount and the European Union formally approved a loan package for U

entries.title: What marijuana's reclassification means for public health and businesses
entries.snippet: The federal government is reclassifying medical marijuana, categorizing it as a drug with potential medical benefits and less potential for harm. While this doesn't legalize marijuana nationally, it does open the door for further research into its effects. William Brangham report

entries.title: 'America First' aid policy reshapes how U.S. delivers global health assistance
entries.snippet: Since the dismantling of USAID, the Trump administration has been revamping aid policies, focusing on smaller deals with recipient governments. Countries receiving American aid will be required to finance part, and eventually, the entire program. Fred de Sam Lazaro reports from K

entries.title: State Department proposes sending Afghans who helped U.S. war effort to Congo
entries.snippet: Hundreds of Afghans who helped the United States' war effort in Afghanistan, and who are detained in Qatar, may soon be sent back to Afghanistan or the Democratic Republic of the Congo by the State Department. The push comes after President Trump halted the Afghan resettlement pr

entries.title: WATCH: Trump says he won't use nuclear weapons in Iran
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said Thursday that he wouldn't use a nuclear weapon in Iran and that nobody should be allowed to use them.

entries.title: 'This is my home,' says DACA recipient deported then returned to U.S.
entries.snippet: The Department of Homeland Security says it has deported more than 675,000 immigrants in the first year of Trump's second term. Although the administration claims to be targeting violent criminals, others continue to be caught in the crackdown, including some who are protected fr

entries.title: Art exhibition shines light on Romani persecution during Holocaust
entries.snippet: It's a lesser-known chapter of the Holocaust, the murder of some 500,000 Roma and Sinti people, members of a long-marginalized and often persecuted minority in Europe. One way into that history is through the work of an artist who survived it herself. Jeffrey Brown reports for ou

entries.title: A brief history of the underground White House bunker at the heart of Trump's ballroom legal case
entries.snippet: Secrecy surrounding White House security makes details hard to come by, but President Donald Trump's court fight over his $400 million ballroom casts some light on an underground bunker at the site that has had a role in history.

entries.title: NTSB says firefighter heard 'stop, stop' before LaGuardia collision but didn't know who it was for
entries.snippet: A firefighter whose truck collided with an Air Canada jet last month on a runway at LaGuardia Airport in New York, killing both pilots, heard an air traffic controller warn "stop, stop, stop" but didn't know who it was for, federal investigators said Thursday.

entries.title: Gunfire inside Mall of Louisiana kills 1 person and wounds 5, Baton Rouge police say
entries.snippet: Two groups exchanged gunfire inside a food court Thursday at the Mall of Louisiana, killing one person and wounding five more, police said.

entries.title: California's woes at the center of debate among leading candidates for governor
entries.snippet: It's the first time in a generation that California has a wide-open contest for the heavily Democratic state's highest office, with more than 50 names on the ballot.

entries.title: Pope urges U.S. and Iran to resume peace talks, condemns capital punishment
entries.snippet: Pope Leo XIV urged the United States and Iran to return to talks to end the war Thursday and condemned capital punishment, in a wide-ranging press conference en route home from his trip to Africa.

entries.title: Federal authorities arrest 2 dozen Mexican Mafia members and associates in California
entries.snippet: More than two dozen members and associates of the Mexican Mafia were arrested Thursday during an early morning crackdown across Southern California, federal authorities said.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Germany news: Lawmakers vote to lower fuel tax amid high gas prices
entries.snippet: People in Germany could see gas prices fall next week after lawmakers approved a bill to lower the fuel tax by €0.17 per liter. Follow DW for these stories and more.

entries.title: Natural molecule could provide Ozempic, Wegovy alternative
entries.snippet: Discovered using AI, a naturally occurring molecule appears to act directly in the brain's appetite center. Scientists hope it could refine treatments for obesity, offering fewer side effects for some patients.

entries.title: German economy growth forecasts halved
entries.snippet: Germany's economy continues to falter under the shock of soaring energy prices. In the wake of the Iran war, the German economy minister has had to cut her own forecast by half.

entries.title: Iran talks should include nuclear experts: EU top diplomat
entries.snippet: Kaja Kallas said failing to bring nuclear experts to the table in talks with Tehran could result in "a more dangerous ⁠Iran." EU leaders meeting in Cyprus are discussing the Middle East conflict, among other topics.

entries.title: Nepal: Mount Everest ice block obstructs large group of spring climbers
entries.snippet: A chunk of ice suspended above the Khumbu Icefall near Base Camp on Mount Everest means it's not safe either for climbers or the specialist "icefall doctors" who typically clear away smaller obstructions.

entries.title: India news: Record turnout in Bengal, Tamil Nadu polls
entries.snippet: West Bengal and Tamil Nadu recorded the highest-ever voter turnout in state polls after controversial voter roll revision. DW has more.

entries.title: Amid fuel crisis, a bold move to leave oil and gas behind
entries.snippet: At an unprecedented meeting in Colombia, delegates from more than 50 countries want to chart a practical path to wean the world off fossil fuels. Can they succeed where the UN climate talks have failed?

entries.title: Thai opposition figures face trial over attempt to change royal defamation law
entries.snippet: Forty-four lawmakers could be banned from office for seeking to change Thailand's strict lese-majeste law, which criminalizes criticism of the monarchy. Critics say it has been used to silence opponents.

entries.title: China's DeepSeek launches preview of new AI model
entries.snippet: DeepSeek, the startup behind a low-cost chatbot that sent shockwaves through the AI community last year, has released its highly anticipated V4 model.

entries.title: Israel, Lebanon to extend ceasefire as US-Iran talks stall
entries.snippet: The announcement came after a rare meeting between Israeli and Lebanese officials at the White House. Meanwhile, the Strait of Hormuz is still closed amid stalled peace talks between the US and Iran. Follow DW.

entries.title: Meta, Microsoft purge jobs amid AI build-up
entries.snippet: Facebook's parent company said it would slash 10% of its workforce, while Microsoft is offering an early retirement scheme. The cuts come as both tech giants make massive investments in AI.

entries.title: US soldier allegedly made $400k on Polymarket betting on Maduro raid
entries.snippet: Prosecutors say the soldier used classified information of the Maduro operation to place winning bets on Polymarket. The case comes amid other incidents of prediction market bets tied to US government actions.

entries.title: ICC says Philippines' Duterte can stand trial over war on drugs
entries.snippet: The ICC has said there is enough evidence linking former Philippines president Rodrigo Duterte to crimes against humanity over his role in orchestrating a deadly anti-drugs crackdown.

entries.title: Warner Bros shareholders greenlight sale to Paramount
entries.snippet: The deal would combine two major Hollywood studios and bring CBS and CNN under one roof, tightening an already concentrated media landscape, critics say.

entries.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
entries.snippet: France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Questions remain about the scope of the operation and whether it can bring lasting stability.

entries.title: Ceasefire extended: What's next in the Iran war?
entries.snippet: With no fixed ceasefire deadline for now, the US and Iran are looking for ways to safeguard their own interests. DW takes a look at the main sticking points and explains some key issues.

entries.title: Is Ethiopia bracing for war?
entries.snippet: Analysts say the war in Iran has temporarily delayed an escalation in smoldering tensions between Ethiopia and Eritrea. But renewed armed conflict cannot be ruled out.

entries.title: Samsung workers rally, call for larger share of AI profits
entries.snippet: Unions at Samsung are threatening a weekslong strike if demands are not met. A production halt at one of the world's largest memory chipmakers could disrupt global chip supplies.

entries.title: UK: Health data volunteered by 500,000 people to Biobank charity listed for sale on Alibaba in China
entries.snippet: The British government is investigating how health data volunteered to a charity for research purposes wound up for sale via three vendors on the Alibaba e-commerce site in China. One listing had 500,000 people's data.
